headline: When it comes to AI nothing's right, Silicon Valley is torn
subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

headline: Death toll in Venezuela quakes rises past 4,000
Ebola outbreak remains out of control, spreads at record speed in the DRC
'Behind the suffering there are people who love life, art and peace': Words of hope from a poetry contest in Gaza
subhead: About 60 students from Gaza, aged 18 to 25, responded to a call from the French Institute of Gaza and the NGO Academic Solidarity with Palestine by submitting poems or videos in which they shared, in French, their feelings and hopes in the face of the war that has affected them for almost three years.

headline: Spain set up World Cup semi-final with France after late win against Belgium
subhead: Spain beat Belgium 2-1, paving the way for a World Cup semi-final against France in Arlington, Texas this coming Tuesday.

headline: 'Foreign Tongues': The Rolling Stones ride a second wave of youth with their new album
subhead: Without reinventing their formula, Mick Jagger and Keith Richards have delivered a catchy and revitalizing record that makes listeners forget the more uneven albums of the past few decades.

headline: Marine Le Pen conviction: 'It's very French to think that preventing one person from running undermines democracy as a whole'
subhead: In the far-right leader's appeals trial, judges once again confirmed the courts' wariness of intervening too heavily in the political arena.

Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.
byline: Lara Pino Lerro and  Romain Imbach

headline: Kanye West, banned from most European countries, receives €4 million grant to perform in Albania
subhead: Barred from entering or performing due to antisemitic and neo-Nazi statements, the American rapper is set to take the stage on July 11 in Tirana. Many protesters are accusing Prime Minister Edi Rama of using the event to serve his own interests.

headline: Eurostar sounds alarm over overcrowding at London's Saint Pancras station
subhead: As rail competition between Paris and London is set to arrive in the 2030s and with Eurostar having ordered new trains, limited station capacity – especially in London – is hampering market growth.

headline: 'Imperial obsession and reckless escalation: Vladimir Putin has never appeared so trapped by his fantasies'
